title: podman 3.0.2-dev / RockyLinux 8.4 / rootless container / no IPV4-Port, only IPV6-Port.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

There are only IPV6-ports open, although IPV6 is disabled in the virtual machine.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run -r -p 8080:80 CONTAINERIMAGE

2. netstat -tulpen
```
tcp6       0      0 :::8080                 :::*                    LISTEN      1000       25198      1575/containers-roo 
```
**Describe the results you received:**
Only IPV6-Ports are open.

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected that podman listens via IPV4.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** always

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Fri Jun 11 04:48:26 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** KVM-VirtualImage

comments.body: Can you access the port via `127.0.0.1:8080`? I think this is an error with `netstat`, `ss -tulpn` lists the port correctly.

Quote from the netstat man page
> NOTES
       This program is mostly obsolete.  Replacement for netstat is ss.  Replacement for netstat -r is ip route.  Replacement for netstat -i is ip -s link.  Replacement for netstat -g is ip maddr.
@ccebinger Do you need more help, I saw your email?
No. It works now for me.
